data.content: The project uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform (via `global.json`). With this runner, TRX reports use `--report-trx`.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet tests with TRX report

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet tests with TRX report

data.reasoningText:
This project uses the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner (as specified in global.json). With this runner, the TRX report is produced using `--report-trx` instead of the traditional `--logger trx` approach.

Let me run the tests.

arguments.description: Run dotnet tests with TRX report

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8d38aa3d5aca476c9eaa074ebbb51600 && dotnet test --report-trx 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet tests with TRX report", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet tests with TRX report

TestProject.csproj

See https://aka.ms/dotnet-test/mtp for more information.

data.content: The project needs to opt into Microsoft.Testing.Platform. I'll update the `.csproj` to enable the MTP runner and add the TRX report extension.
data.reasoningText:
The project needs to opt into Microsoft.Testing.Platform. With MSTest 3.8.0, we need to add `<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>` and set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` in the csproj, or use the `<UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>` property.

Actually, with MSTest 3.8.0 and the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner, you need to set the `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>` property. Let me check what's needed.
 
The global.json specifies Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the runner, but the project is still using VSTest. For MSTest SDK v3.3 and later, I can enable the platform by setting either `<UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>` or the newer `<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>` with `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`.

Since we're on MSTest 3.8.0, I should add `<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>`, `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, and `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>` to the project file to properly enable the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner.

For TRX report generation, I'll need to add the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` package separately since the base MSTest meta-package doesn't include it by default. Let me start with the properties and add the package reference if needed.

data.reasoningText: Hmm, it's still saying the project is using the VSTest runner. Let me check the dotnet version and also look at the exact MSBuild properties that are being evaluated.

arguments.description: Check dotnet version and MTP property

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8d38aa3d5aca476c9eaa074ebbb51600 && dotnet --version 2>&1 && dotnet msbuild -getProperty:IsTestingPlatformApplication TestProject.csproj 2>&1", "description": "Check dotnet version and MTP property", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.description: Check dotnet version and MTP property

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-8d38aa3d5aca476c9eaa074ebbb51600 && dotnet restore 2>&1 && dotnet msbuild -getProperty:IsTestingPlatformApplication TestProject.csproj 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The `IsTestingPlatformApplication` property is `false`. With MSTest 3.8.0, this should be automatic when `EnableMSTestRunner` is set. Let me check if the MSTest package is restoring properly.

Actually, wait. With .NET 10 and MSTest 3.8.0, maybe the behavior changed. Let me check what properties are needed. Let me look at this differently - maybe I need to use `<UseVSTest>false</UseVSTest>` or something else.

Actually, I think the issue might be that with newer versions of MSTest, `EnableMSTestRunner` might have been replaced or the integration works differently. 

Let me try running a restore first to make sure the packages are pulling down correctly, then I'll check if the property gets set as expected.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-8d38aa3d5aca476c9eaa074ebbb51600 && dotnet restore 2>&1 && dotnet msbuild -getProperty:IsTestingPlatformApplication TestProject.csproj 2>&1

Actually, let me check if the `Microsoft.Testing.Platform.MSBuild` package is even being referenced. With MSTest 3.8.0, this should be included, but maybe the `EnableMSTestRunner` property needs to be evaluated before the package is resolved.

Let me try setting `<IsTestingPlatformApplication>true</IsTestingPlatformApplication>` directly.

arguments.description: Search for compatible TrxReport package versions

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Search for compatible TrxReport package versions

data.reasoningText:
There seems to be a version incompatibility between dotnet 10.0.100 and the MSTest/TrxReport packages. The handshake protocol version ('8') isn't supported. This is likely because the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` version 1.6.2 doesn't support .NET 10.

Let me try a different approach:
1. Maybe I need a newer version of the TrxReport package
2. Or maybe I should try running the test executable directly instead of through `dotnet test`

Let me first check what versions of the packages are available and compatible with .NET 10.
